old_claim.note: Glass-Blowing Workshops provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because glass tesserae are mosaic materials and do not require vessel-oriented blown-glass workshop production.

source_shape.source_locator: Roman Glass says blown glass did not supplant cast glass, and separately lists multicolored glass tesserae as applications of Roman glass in floor and wall mosaics.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source separates Roman glassblowing from other glass production methods and identifies mosaic tesserae as one use of Roman glass.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Because the source shows Roman glass uses and production routes were broader than blowing, glass-blowing workshops are too narrow to be a direct prerequisite for glass mosaic tesserae.

ontology_before: Glass Mosaic Tesserae depended directly on Glass-Blowing Workshops, implying blown-vessel workshop production enabled the mosaic tesserae material.
ontology_after: Glass Mosaic Tesserae depends on glassmaking and mosaic art; glass-blowing workshops remain adjacent Roman glass production infrastructure, not a prerequisite.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: glass_mosaic_tesserae no longer directly depends on glass_blowing_workshops.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed specifically to tesserae produced only by glass-blowing workshops.
A stronger source shows Roman glass tesserae were normally manufactured through blown-vessel workshop processes.
The graph adds a specialized blown_glass_tesserae node where glass-blowing workshops are the exact production context.
short_reason: Glass-blowing workshops are too narrow for mosaic glass tesserae production.
